prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response:
Verily, the quarterly spoils of the SaaS guild reveal a curious harvest, wherein the merchants doth peddle invisible scrolls encoded with arcane magics called "software," which groweth in number like enchanted rabbits upon the green fields of the cloud.

Lo, the tomes declare an increase in gold coins by the sum of fourteen thousand three hundred and seventy, though none may discern whether these riches cometh from the honest townsfolk or by summoning creatures named "subscriptions" that renew themselves without cease.

Strangely, the scribes who labor behind glowing glass panes speak of a realm called "user engagement," as if their patrons be bewitched to cast spells upon mystical devices fashioned from crystal and metal, known to all as "smartphones."

In conclusion, good lords and ladies, the ledger teacheth that our SaaS knights have vanquished the quarter with valor, even as we ponder what manner of sorcery allows customers to abide perpetually in this invisible market, purchasing naught but ethereal promises.
